nested_cv_reference.note: Nested CV is the rigorous estimate; per-sample predictions are from a single train/test split or resubstitution

discrepancy_analysis.explanation: The optimized panel numbers (AUC=0.896, sens=69.5%) come from LASSO fit on the FULL dataset — this is training performance, not generalization performance. The nested CV estimate (AUC=0.871, sens=47.7%) is the honest estimate. The 69.5% claim is ~22 percentage points optimistic compared to nested CV.

new_threshold_0_20.note: Training performance — needs nested CV to confirm

threshold_comparison.key_finding: Lowering delta-beta threshold from 0.30 to 0.20 recovered more CpG signatures (42K discovery, 37K validated) but the OPTIMAL PANEL REMAINS THE SAME 7 PROTEIN MARKERS. No methylation markers entered the cost-weighted LASSO because methylation cost is set to $75/marker. The apparent sensitivity improvement (47.7% → 69.5%) is an artifact of comparing nested CV vs training performance, NOT a real improvement from threshold lowering.

methylation_entry_point.diagnosis: Overfit — n=24 methylation samples cannot support this many features

methylation_exclusion.root_cause: Two compounding problems: (1) Methylation cost set to $75/marker, but targeted bisulfite panels cost $50-100 total for ~20 CpGs (~$5/CpG). The cost penalty is ~15x too high. (2) The methylation training data has only n=24 samples. When methylation features enter the model they immediately overfit (AUC=1.0), making the LASSO path uninformative for finding the right methylation panel size.

corrected_cost_estimate.expected_impact: With corrected costs, methylation CpGs should enter the LASSO path earlier, but overfitting will still be a problem with n=24

a_protein_new_threshold.note: Same 7 markers as old threshold — threshold change didn't affect protein selection

b_protein_old_threshold.markers: Same 7 proteins (MMP9, CXCL8, S100A4, TIMP1, SEPT9, ERBB2, SPP1)
b_protein_old_threshold.note: Identical panel, old cost estimate was higher

c_mixed_forced_methylation.warning: AUC=0.9997 and sens=100% on n=24 methylation samples is CLASSIC OVERFITTING. These numbers are not reliable.
c_mixed_forced_methylation.note: Cannot be trusted until validated on independent cohort

panel_comparisons.summary: The protein panel is identical at both thresholds. The 'improvement' from threshold lowering is a measurement artifact. The mixed panel with forced methylation shows perfect performance — which is a red flag, not a success. All methylation-containing panels need independent validation.

recovered_cpgs_contribution.note: Threshold lowering from 0.30 to 0.20 recovered CpGs from BMP3, MGMT, RASSF1 (APC borderline)
recovered_cpgs_contribution.impact_on_classifier: NONE for protein panel — these are methylation markers, protein panel unchanged
recovered_cpgs_contribution.impact_on_methylation_classifier: The methylation-only classifier already uses 298 cMCD-gene CpGs. The recovered CpGs from BMP3/MGMT/RASSF1 are included in this pool. Top selected features (cg22418909, cg20611276, etc.) need mapping to genes to determine if recovered CpGs are among the 20 selected features.
recovered_cpgs_contribution.developer_action_needed: Map top 20 selected CpGs to gene names to identify if recovered BMP3/MGMT/RASSF1 CpGs contribute
